title: Sortable List
description:
Build a **Sortable List** component where items can be moved up and down using buttons.

### Requirements

- Render an ordered list of items passed via the `items` prop
- Each item displays its label, a **Move Up** button, and a **Move Down** button
- Clicking Move Up swaps the item with the one above it
- Clicking Move Down swaps the item with the one below it
- The Move Up button on the **first** item must be **disabled**
- The Move Down button on the **last** item must be **disabled**
- Support an `onChange` callback that receives the reordered array after every move
- Items maintain their identity during reordering (use the `id` field as the key)

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| List container | `sortable-list` |
| Item row at index N | `item-{index}` (0-indexed, position-based) |
| Move Up for index N | `move-up-{index}` |
| Move Down for index N | `move-down-{index}` |
constraints:
- Use React functional components with hooks
- Do not use any drag-and-drop libraries
- The list must re-render efficiently after each swap
hints: Store the items array in state so you can reorder it.
To swap two items, create a copy of the array and use destructuring assignment: [arr[i], arr[j]] = [arr[j], arr[i]].
Use the position-based index for data-testid, not the item's id.
Remember to call onChange after updating the state.
